title: Advent of Europeans
content:
EUROPEANS

> Context
Why was India important?
 * Trade -
    Cotton (Sindon) {native to India}, Ivory, Spices like Black pepper and cardamom{both native to India}
 * Grape wine in Europe that there is a rich Christian king in India.

Arabs were natural trade partners to India.{due to monsoon helping in travel}
Europeans wanted to cut the Arab intermediaries.
From Spain, Columbus stated his journey to find India but found Caribbean islands (he named West Indies) and later America.
Spain and Portugal got into Treaty of Tordesillas -> an imaginary line to demark exploration. {left -> Spain and right -> Portugal}; They did not consider any other kingdom :)
1498 - Portuguese come to India.
Indian society was: Conservative -> Progressive, Feudalistic -> capitalistic, Exclusive -> Inclusive social system
Reason for this change -> Self assertion based on Introspection -> External stimuli of Idea's


# INTRODUCTION

Adventure in the sea led to establishment of new global order. European kingdoms started to promote sea fairing activity to earn profit from trade with the eastern world.
In this endeavor, Portuguese were the first successful people. Portuguese sailor Vasco da Gama was 1st to find direct sea route to India in 1498. For about a century, Portuguese dominated Indian trade but in the beginning of the 17th century other European companies started to arrive in India.

Portuguese were followed by Dutch -> English -> Danes -> French

Since the arrival of 1st European in 1498, and till the independence of India in 1947 much water has flown under the bridge (much changed), which changed the shape and discourse of Indian history and society.

India got transformed from the status of a feudalistic, conservative and exclusive social system to a capitalistic, progressive and inclusive social system.
This change has happened due to self assertion based on introspection and external stimuli of ideas like freedom, liberty and equality etc.
As such India got transformed from One mans rule to peoples rule i.e. Monarchy to Democracy.


# PORTUGUESE

(not a company but an official convoy of their government)
Objective: God, Gold, and Glory.
 * Portuguese King Henry (the navigator) took lead among the European countries to promote sea fairing activities. This promotion was meant to secure profits from trade with the eastern world. and also to promote Christianity (the purpose was God, Gold, and Glory)
 * During the tenure the King John ll, a sailor named Barthelomew Diaz reached the Cape of good hope in 1488. During the tenure of king Imanuel, a sailor named Vasco Da Gama started his journey in July 1497 and reached cape of Good hope. From there he traveled to Malindi (Somalia), where he got the help of Gujarati pilot Abdul Majid and finally in May 1498 he landed at the coast of Calicut in Kerala.
 * In Calicut, he was welcomed by local Zamorin ruler {Samuthiri}. VDG stayed in Calicut for 4 months and purchased huge cargo from Kerala. On his return to Portugal he sold these commodities on 60 times benefit.
 * In 1501, another sailor named Pedro Alvarez Cabral visited Calicut but he started confronting the Arab traders {because of trade rivalry + Muslims were arch rivals of Christians}. Moreover he also vascodagama burnt a ship with huge number of people returning from Mecca. It led to development of acrimony with the Calicut ruler. Cabrel was driven away from Calicut.
   Cabrel befriended adversary of Calicut -> Ruler of cochin and Cannanore.
 * In 1503 once again Vasco da Gama visited India. he tried to restore peace with Calicut and went on to create 1st Portuguese factory in India i.e. at Calicut. After the return of VDG, Portuguese govt. decided to sent their official envoy to India i.e. Portuguese viceroy


> 1st Portuguese Viceroy -> Francisco-de-Almedia (1505-09)
FdA said to the Portuguese king

> Instead of creating factories and protecting them, lets focus on our Navel forces -> this will eliminate the constant threat of Arab merchants --> Therefore launched Blue Water Policy (to strengthen Portuguese Navy in the Indian Ocean)

He maintained to the Portuguese government that rather than building forts and factories on land, Portuguese must strengthening their navy in the Indian ocean and Arabian Sea. That's how they will surpass the Arab traders influence in India.

In this regard Almedia initiated Blue Water policy to strengthen the Navy. In 1508 he faught with the alliance of Gujarat + Calicut ruler in Battle of Chaul, where he was defeated {His son was also killed in this}. But next year in 1509, he defeated the Alliance rulers.

> Albuquerque (1509-1510)
He is regarded as the real founder of Portuguese power in India. He established Portuguese control in Strait of Malacca and Strait of Hormuz and as such they started to control the entire trade of Indian Ocean, Arabian Sea, and BoB. He also started Cartaze System, whereby a permit fee or license fee was charged on the other ships which were passing through the routes.

In 1510, he also captured Goa from the Bijapuri ruler. Goa later became political HQ of the Portuguese.

> Nino-Da-Cunha (1529-38) {last important viceroy}

During his tenure, Goa became the capital of Portuguese. He captured the area of Diu from the Gujarati ruler Bahadur Shah. Bassein also became part of Portuguese territory. In the later time by 1560 Portuguese further expanded to Daman and Hoogly.

>> Influence of Portuguese in India
 1. They introduced Printing Press in India in 1556 at Goa. The initial purpose was to publish bible but later other literature were also published.
 2. Portuguese were the 1st European who started writing about benefits of Indian medicinal herbs.
 3. They introduced many of the new world plantation{Americas were called as new world, eastern region as old world} in India eg: Tobacco, Potato{origin: South America}, Tomato{o:Mexico}, chillis{o:Mexico}, new variety of Pineapple and coconut. They introduced Cashew plantations for the purpose of land reclamation and stopping the leaching of soil and checking soil erosion.
 4. A missionary from Portugal, St. Xavier established the Jesuit Order in India. {The church is present in Goa -> bomb of basilica}. He popularized this order and in 1546 he died on a Chinese island.
 5. They promoted marriage with the Indian community to further develop trust.
 6. They banned Sati in their controlled areas.

Order of creation: British EIC -> 1600, Dutch EIC -> 1602
Order of Arrival: British EIC -> 1608, Dutch EIC -> 1605
* Cinnamon is native spice of Sri lanka


# DUTCH EIC

{symbol: VoC, 1st multi-national, they first arrived in Indonesia (native spice -> Clove, Nutmeg and Mace)}

It was created in 1602 and was the 1st multinational company in the world. They arrived in India in 1605 and created their 1st warehouse in Masulipatnam in 1605 itself. Before coming to India, they already increased their presence in Indonesia and created their settlements in Batam and Batavia(Jakarta). In India, their expansion happened in quick fashion. They created their warehouses at Cochin, Pulicat, Surat, Balasore, Nagapattanam, Chinsura, Qasim Baazar, Dacca, Patna, Bimlipatnam etc.

Dutch completely replaced Portuguese from Cochin. {Why?}
 * They also started to release the coins in the name of Lord Venkatashwar.
 * They adopted the policy of respect towards Indian culture.

But in the middle of the 18th century, a tripartite struggle started between french, English and dutch to establish the monopoly of trade in India. In 1759, British EIC defeated Dutch EIC in the battle of Bidara/Chinsura/Hooghly. They were thoroughly defeated after which they decided to focus on Indonesian islands rather than fighting with other Europeans in India. Slowly they stated to wind up their operation in India and by 1825 they completely left India.


# BRITISH EIC

In 1575, Queen Elisabeth made statement
> Ships make History; -> if British has to be great, they have to be great in Naval power as well

In 1608, William Hawkins came to Jahangir's court -> Jahangir was impressed with him -> got permission to built factory in Surat -> Portuguese weren't happy with this. (Mughal's sea trade was carried on by Portuguese) -> Clash between P and B (J did not intervene) -> In 1612 Thomas best defeated the Portuguese
-> Jahangir fully got impressed with Britishers.

In 1599, Merchant Adventurers of the East organization was created to trade with the eastern nations and earn profit from the trade. On 31st Dec 1600, Queen Elisabeth of Britain gave a Royal Charter to the company for 15 years to trade with eastern nations.
Later, the charter was extended for an infinite period till further notice. Over the period this company came to be known as British EIC or English EIC.
The 1st official envoy of company led by Sir William Hawkins visited Mughal Emperer Jahangir's Court. Impressed by William Hawkins he gave him a Mansabdar Position and British were further allowed to create their warehouse at Surat and Masulipatnam.

The very presence of British in Mughal court was not liked by Portuguese which led to fight between the two. When Hawkins did not get any support from the Mughals, he left India.

In 1612, the British envoy led by Thomas best defeated Portuguese in Battle of Swalley. Jahangir was impressed by this.
{Noor Jahan(wife of J; aspirational lady:tf wanted influence in Mughal court) -> supporting B's} {the Painting story of Jahangir}

In 1615, a British envoy led by Sir Thomas Roe visited Jahangir's court. At this time, J gave all the necessary permission to B's to create further warehouses in India. Till this date, this was the biggest achievement in the mughal court and after it there was no looking back for B in India.

They created their warehouses at Bharuch, Ahmedabad, Agra before 1620. In 1633, they created a trading center at Harharpur in Eastern India. In 1639, B officer Francis Day got the village of madras on lease from the ruler of Chandragiri. In Madras, B created St. Fort George, which was their 1st fort in India.

In 1661, there was a matrimonial alliance b/n Portuguese princess Catherine of Bryganza and British prince Charles II. The island of Bombay was transferred to British crown as a dowry price. Later in 1668, Bombay was transferred to BEIC on a nominal price of 10 pounds. Soon Bombay became the business HQ of B's in India.

Entry of B in Bengal happened in 1650's. It was Mughal governer Shah Suja, who allowed British to create their habitation at Hooghly. But in 1686, due to some local dispute, with the Mughal Governors, B's did not paid the taxes. After which Aurangzeb snatched all the privileges from Hoooghly.

At this time a B officer Job Charnock persuaded Aurangzeb as such in 1690. Aurangzeb restored B privileges in Bengal on an annual payment of 3000 rupees. In 1698, B's got 3 villages on lease i.e. Gobindpur, sutanati, Kalikatta. They clubbed these villages and created St Fort William calcutta. Which later became political HQ and the capital of B India.


# DANISH EIC

> not big power in India, interested in Missionary activity.
This company was created in 1616 and their main obj was to propagate Christianity. Initially, they established their 1st settlement at Tranquebar, TN. Later by 1676 they created their settlement Fredrick Nagar in Serampore area of Bengal.

In 1799, Danish officer William Riverend Carry established a printing press in Serampore area. it's purpose was to publish bible and propagate Christianity. in 1819, Reverend Carry founded serampore college for western style higher education in India.

By 1845 they sold all their properties to B and winded up their operations in India.


# FRENCH EIC

Name of Pondicherry: Arikmedu → Poduka → Pondicherry
It was the last European company which came to India. it was created in 1664 and this company was not a private entity but owned by the french government. In 1723, the control of the French government became 100%. Their 1st warehouse was at Surat (1668), Masulipatnam (1669), Pondicherry (1673) -> {initially settlement was created by Portuguese}, chandranagar (1690) in Bengal.

Over the period pondicherry emerged as political HQ of french in India. French Governor, Joseph Francois Dupleix started the strategy of interfering in the political affairs of the native kingdoms of India. To gain political milage in carnatic area. Very soon BEIC started to follow in the footsteps of French and they also started interfering in the political affairs of native kingdoms.

It led to bitter fight between French and British, which continued for the next 20 years. These wars are popularly known as Carnatic Wars in which finally BEIC was victorious, which led to end of french ambition to create a colonial empire in India.




LATER MUGHAL'S

After the death of Aurangzeb, war of succession started among the sons of Aurangzeb. In which prince Muazzam defeated and killed two of his brothers Azam and Kambaksha as such the 65 year old prince Muazzam ascended to the throne with the title Bahadur Shah 1 or Shah Alam 1.

Bahadur Shah 1 (1707-1712)
He was learned personality. Though he was very good in diplomacy but not a strong military administrator. He tried to reverse the religious divisive policies of Aurangzeb and offered peace to many communities.
 * He offered peace to Rajputs, Jats and Bundelas.
 * He stopped the destruction of Hindu Temples.
 * He also released Maratha Prince Sahu from the Mughal captivity, who was kept prisoner since 1689. {This release led to the succession struggle among the Marathas i.e. b/n Sahu and Tarabai; It was a strategic move by him as under the leadership of Tarabai, Marathas were gaining the power}
 * He also offered a very high mansab position to the Sikh guru Guru Govind Singh, but soon after the death of guru, Sikh community refused to accept any peace with Mughals. They decided that they will continue their struggle with the Mughals under the leadership of Banda Bahadur.

Bahadur Shah often granted lavish Jagir to many of his nobles without any merit. Because of it he was called as Shah-i-Bekhabar (The headless king) -> Reason: He didn't want any conflict within his rule.
After the death of him, his sons were in race of grabbing power so that they left his body unburied for over a month.

Jahandar Shah (1712-13)
He came to power with the support of an Iranian noble Zulfiqar Khan. As a token of gratitude, position of wazir was given to him. Jahandar Shah was a degenerate person, fully engaged in the physical pleasure as such all the decisions were take by Wazir. He took following initiatives on the behalf of emperor:
 * He abolished the discriminatory Jizya Tax {It was imposed on non-Muslims and purpose was to provide protection to community} {Akbar -> Abolished it; Aurangzeb -> Reimposed it}
 * He initiated IZARA system of taxation. Under this, tax collection authority was chosen by bidding process. the highest bidder was chosen and given the contract of collecting the revenue from a particular area. They were popularly known as Izaredars. Very often this system of taxation led to exploitation & excruciation of taxpayers.
 * It was also decided that Marathas would be given Chauth and Sardeshmukhi of the deccan. But it was not implemented during the tenure of Jahandar shah and later it was implemented during the period of Farrukhsiyar.

→ Chauth: It is 1/4th of the total revenue collection of the Deccan.
→ Sardeshmukhi: it was 10% additional levy on chauth which was to be given to Marathas.
Farrukhsiyar (1713-19)
→ 4 type of ethnic nobles were present: Turkish, Iranian, Afghan, and Indian
He came to power due to support of Hindustani nobles Sayyid Brothers. In return, Abdulla sayyid was given the position of Wazir and Hasan Ali khan as Mir Bakshi (Commander in Chief of the forces). Sayyid brothers adopted tolerant attitude towards the Indian community and started to include large number of Hindu Chiefs in Mughal Administration. OTOH, conflict with Sikh leader Banda Bahadur continued and he was arrested from Gurudaspur and later executed at Delhi in 1716.

It is said that Farrukhsiyar was suffering from some disease and he enlisted the help of British physician William Hamilton who performed a minor surgery of Farrukhsiyar. Impressed by the company's services, he granted Royal Farman to Company.
Royal Farman of 1717 to EIC
 1. Company was allowed to perform duty free trade in Bihar, Bengal and Odisha.
 2. Company was further granted a lease of 38 villages to fortify Calcutta further.
 3. Company was also allowed to mint its own coin in Bengal.
 4. Though the private trade of company employees was allowed but they had to pay all the duties. {It was highly misused provision as such company employees often misuse Royal Dastak/Certificates to sell the private product as company products}

→ MQK was not happy with the point 2,3 -> So he setup successor state of Bengal.
Farrukhsiyar grew suspicious of sayyid brothers and started planning their death. Once sayyid brothers learned this news, they took help of Maratha Peshwa Balaji Vishwanath. first they removed Farrukhsiyar from power, blinded him and finally he was assassinated.
In quick succession Rafi-ud-darjat and Rafi-ud-daulah were appointed as the next emperor but unfortunately they did not survive. {were too happy to be emperor.

By this point of time Sayyiad brothers has emerged as the king makers in the mughal history. Finally in 1719, Muhammad Shah was appointed as next emperor.

Muhammad Shah (1719-1748) {Long tenure but unstable rule}

After coming to power, he wanted to remove sayyid brothers. At this time all the other noble groups under the leadership of Asaf Jah supported the emperor as such by 1722, one by one both sayyid brotjers were eliminated. As a token of gratitude Asaf Jah was given the position of wazir. He was a highly capable person and he wanted to reform the corrupt mughal administration. But the emperor, MS was the not the man of the moment. Mohammand Shah was often indulged in carnal pleasures. He was highly fond of wine and women as such he was popularly called as Rangeela.

Due to his inept rule, the irreversible decline of the Mughal kingdom started. Emperor also started to doubt the intention of Asaf Jah. Frustrated with the suspicious nature of the emperor, Asaf Jah decided to quit the position of wazir and he started to follow his own aspiration. By this point of time, already the breakup of the Mughal empire has started.
 * In 1717, Bengal became semi-independent under Murshid Qli Khan
 * in 1722, Awadh under the leadership of Saadat Khan
 * In 1724, Hyderabad under Asaf Jah

In 1739, ruler of Persia, Nadir Shah invaded mughal India which led to Battle of Karnal in 1739. Mughal Army was thoroughly defeated in this battle. Moreover, to take the revenge of killing of some of his soldiers, Nadir Shah entered into royal city of Delhi and performed a terrible massacre on the streets of Delhi. He also looted the most priced possessions of Mughals eg: Pecock Throne of Shah Jahan and Kohinoor Diamond.

After death of Nadir shah his successor Ahmad Shah Abdali started the invasion of India in b/n 1748 to 1767, He invaded India 7 times.

Ahmad Shah (1748-54) {do not confuse it with the Abdali}
During his tenure Abdali captured Punjab and Multan from the Mughal rulers. He also appointed his agents to look after the administration of Punjab.

Alamgir II (1754-59)
By the time of Alamgir, the Marathas have become more powerful and they started their northern campaign. Marathas expelled agents of Abdali from Punjab and occupied Lahore. It became major bone of contention b/n Abdali and Marathas which led to 3rd Battle of Panipat in 1761.

Shah Jahan III (1759-60)

Shah Alam II (1760-1806)
He was the man of some ability and some courage but his problem was Mughal glory was beyond redemption. He came to power with the support of Maratha leader Sadashiv Rao Bhau. In 3rd battle of pnaipat, Shah alam supported marathas as such after his victory abdili exiled Shah alam from delhi for 12 years and mughal emperor started to live in Allahabad.

Meanwhile the administration of delhi was organised by agents of abdili -> Rohilla's {Najibullah Khan, Zabita Khan}. In 1771, Maratha leader, Mahadji Schindia overran delhi and drove our Rohillas from delhi. once again shah alam was brought back to delhi's throne. In 1788, one rohilla leader Gulam Qadir, entered the delhi in night, looted the wealth of people and also brutally blinded emperor Shah Alam. Once again Mahadji Schindia came to the rescue of Mughal monarch and hunted down Ghulam Qadir.

The blind emperor continued till 1806 but by this time the directly controlled area under mughal was just 20kms.

Akbar II (1806-37)
He gave the title of "Raja" to famous social reformer Ram Mohan Roy. During his tenure BEIC stopped minting coins in the name of Mughal Monarch and now coins were minted with the name of British Monarch. They also replaced the official language from Persian to English.

Bahadur Shah II / Bahadur Shah Jafar (1837-58)
He was the last mughal monarch who wrote urdu poetry with the pen name Jafar. During his tenure the mighty revolt of 1857 happened. It was organized in his name. After the suppression of revolt BSJ was deported to Rangoon, where his death happened in 1861.

On 1st Nov 1858, At a durbar held at Allahabad, British Gov Gen Canning read the proclamation of Queen Vicrotia which formally ended the mughal rule and led to the beginning of British crown.

> Mughal Administration

King -> Wazir -> Nobility (Turkish, Persian, Afghan, Hindustani)

-> Mansabdari System
 1. It is derived from the word "Mansab" which means "official Position". It was initiated by Akbar.
 2. All the big officers of the empire were generally reffered as "Mansabdar".
 3. This system has two important components:
    1. Zat: It indicated official rank of a mansabdar in hierarchy. The rank of mansabdar was indicated through numerical value which ranged from 500 to 5000 in nominal terms. In exceptional terms 7000 and 11000 ranks were also given. A.T. rank salary was paid to the mansabdar. Those who got cash salary are k/as Naqdi Mansabdar. Those who were given land grant were k/as Jagirdari Mansabdar. There were two types of Jagir i.e. Tankhwah Jagir -> given for temporary period and Watan Jagir -> It was permanent and hereditary.
    2. Sawar: A.T. Rank and salary, a mansabdar has to maintain particular number of soldiers and horses in the service of mughal monarch.
       

Q: To what extent was Jagirdari crisis responsible for decline of Mughal?
Q: Discuss the factors responsible for the decline of Mughals?
Not only Jagirdari crisis but other reasons were responsible for the decline. These factors can be divided into political, economic, socio-religious, and military nature.

 * Political Causes:
   * The expansionist policy of Aurangzeb sapped the vitality of the mugal empire. {very expensive; it was deccan campaign -> Marathwada, Bijapur and Golconda; never was able to beat shivaji} {shivaji was beaten by Jai singh I in the battle of purandar 1665}
   * Aurangzeb did not respected the line of succession and after his death there was a chaos in mughal politics due to no clear line of succession.
   * After the death of Aurangzeb there was heavy political instability. In just 12 years 6 rulers were appointed to the mughal throne.
   * Majority of the mughal emperors were incapable and some of them did not have character to rule. Many of them indulged in physical pleasure rather than administration.
   * Mughal Wazirs became very powerful in this duration and there was intense competition among the nobility to get the position of Wazir.
   * A court factionalism{groupism based on ethnic identity} also emerged in the mughal court and the cooperation between different noble groups completely declined
     
 * Economic causes
   * Jagirdari Crisis: At the core of mughal administration was Mansabdari system. The mughal might depended on the strength of its mansabdars. M'ds played a vital role in the expansion of mughal empire so as their own income.
     * Aurangzeb initiated very long drawn expensive wars in Marathwada, Bijapur and Golconda. After establishing his control in these areas, he declared majority of land in royal category and less land was made available for granting purpose. {initially it was 80%->M'ds and 20%->emperor, but in this expedition it was >50% for emperor}
     * Moreover, the jagirs were highly disproportionate, small and unequal in size. It led to intense fight b/n the Mughal Jagirdars to increase their land holding. It is called as Jagirdari crisis in the Mughal court.
     * Many of the mughal jagirdar tried to extract more taxes, which often led to revolt and it further led to erosion of trust in mughal administration.
       
 * Socio-Religious Causes
   * The religious divisive policy of Aurangzeb played a vital role in decline
   * The forcible religious conversions were highly detested by majority population.
   * Aurangzeb destroyed large number of temples including somnath temple, kashi vishwanath, keshav rai temple, Govind dev Temple of mathura-vrindavan(built by Raja man singh -> Navratna of Akbar's court, Akbar also paid large sum to build this)
   * A'zb banned many festivals like Holi, Muharram(Shia festival, he was of sunni faith), Navroz
   * He reimposed Jiziya Tax, which was abolished by Akbar.
     
 * Military causes of decline
   
   * 18th century India witnessed the consolidation of European powers in India. Though they were not great in numbers but they had highly upgraded military technology. OTOH, there was no change in the mughal army and they were still fighting with old weapon and technology. Once they faced the opponent like british they proved to be highly inferior.

These kingdoms were emerged after the death of Aurangzeb.

Successor States <- <- Mughal Empire -> -> Rebellious States

 1. Successor States: These were semi-independent and completely autonomous states. These states were created by the erstwhile nobility of mughals. They did not cutoff their complete links with mughals and they respected the nominal leadership of mughal emperor. They sent annual tributes to mughal monarch. They also continues minting coins in the name of mughal monarch and friday prayers were also raised in his name.
    * Eg: Bengal {1717}, Awadh {1722}, Hyderabad {1724}
      
 2. Rebellious States: These states were independent kingdoms but during the tenure of Aurangzeb some of their areas were annexed and captured. After the death of Aurangzeb the rulers of these areas re-establlished their complete independence and autonomy. They didn't respected nominal leadership of mughals and they were not supposed to mint coins in the name of mughal monarch. They were also not supposed to sent any tribute to the mughal emperor.
    * Eg: Mysore, Maratha and Punjab

Rajput's belonged to neither of them. They were allied kingdoms since the rule of Akbar, and even in later Mughals we saw steps taken by emperor to have good relationship with them.


ANGLO-FRENCH RIVALRY (CARNATIC WARS)

-> There are 4 main players in this -> B's, French, Nizams, and carnatic

Hyderabad
 * Hyderabad was one of the rich province of Mughal India. In between 1713-1722, Chin-Qilich Khan was the Governor of Hyd. In 1722, he became Mughal wazir. He wanted to reform corrupt mughal administration but due to very suspicious nature of mughal monarch. He resigned from the position of wazir. He decided to pursue his own ambition. In 1724, he defeated and killed Mubariz Khan in Battle of kheda and established Asaf Jahi Dynasty in Hyd.
 * His successors came to be known as Nizams of Hyderabad.

Carnatic
 * Carnatic was the coromandal coast of India and its hinterland. In 1690, Aurangzeb recognised Nawabship of carnatic. Carnatic was kept under the legal purview of Hyderabad Governor. In 1724, when hyderabad became semi-independent autonomous state, automatically carnatic also became free from Mughal control.
 * Carnatic nawab Saadat-allah-khan started to take his own decisions without any interference of Mughal emperor of his nobles.
 * After his death, Dost Ali became the next carnatic nawab.

Background:
 * In 1740, after the death of Dost Ali war of succession started in carnatic. At this time, French governor Dupliex decided to interfere in the political affairs of carnatic so as to get a greater political milage in Carnatic. Very soon, BEIC also followed in the footsteps of french. They started to interfare in Carnatic politics.
 * It led to development in bitter fight between B's and F'h which continued for more than 20 years. These fights are referred as Carnatic Wars.

1st Anglo-French War (1742-46):
 * In 1740, Austrian War of Succession started in Europe after the death of King of Hapsburg Empire Carles Vth. The two contenders for powers were daughter of King charles Maria Theresa and another was Charles Albert. B's supported daughter's claim and french supported Charles Albert which led to war of succession.
 * Both the companies also started to fight not only in India but also in North America. French Gov Dupliex attacked on Madras. At this time B's sent help message to Nawab of Carnatic Md. Anwaruddin. But both B's and Carnatic forces were thoroughly defeated in Madras by French.
 * OTOH, in NA, B's captured french area of Nova Scottia and Breron Island.
 * This war ended with Treaty of Aix-La-Chappel.
 * As per the treaty, Madras was restored to the b's and F'h got back their territories in NA.

2nd A-F War/Carnatic War (1749-1754)
 * In 1749, French defeated and killed Nawab of Carnatic Anwaruddin in Battle of Ambur. It led to war of succession b/n Md. Ali(B) and Chanda Sahib(F)
 * In Hyderabad, death of Asaf Jah happened in 1748, which led to war of succession b/n Nasir Jung(B) and Muzaffar Jung (F)
 * Finally, French were victorious in installing their candidates at both places. Initially the B's attitude was very callous and they accepted their defeat but soon after the appointment of new General Saunder's they felt to to put up some resistance against french.
 * At this point of time a young clerk Robert Clive volunteered to fight and he also suggested alternative strategy to defeat french. Saunders gave a chance to Robert Clive.
   * Seige of Arcot: With a small section of British force RC entered into carnatic capital arcot when, CS was marching towards Trichonapally(Trichy) {to kill Md.A}. In turn of events, Chanda sahib has to return back to Arcot{When the news of the capture of Arcot reached Chanda Saheb} but in his own capital he was besieged by B forces and finally defeated and killed. This was the 1st victory of B against Carnatic Nawab and French. The war ended with the treaty of Pondicherry in 1754, as per the treaty French were forced to recall Dupleix from India, and the French were allowed to retain their influence on Hyderabad for time being.

3rd A-F Battle/carnatic War (1756-1763)
 * In 1756, a seven-year war broke out in Europe which renewed the Anglo-French rivalry all across the world to establish their imperial dominance (Hegemony).
 * In India, the most important and decisive battle was fought in 1760 known as the battle of Wandiwash.
 * In this battle, British officer Sir Eyre Coote thoroughly defeated French general Count de Lally. and it ended with treaty of paris (1763)
   * Treaty of Paris:
     * As per the treaty, French were allowed to retain all their territories in India created prior to 1749.
     * Further allowed to continue trade and commerce in India but they had to accept the British Protectorate in India.
     * It winded up the imperialist ambition in India to create a colonial empire in India and B's emerged as sole colonial power in India.

Factors responsible for French defeat
 * French was a Government-owned company which made it slow in terms of decision making. On the other hand, British was a private company and decision-making was fast.
 * French faced financial difficulties because it needed many approvals from French authorities. On the other hand, the British did not face any financial difficulty. After the Plassey plunder, ready finances were available at the expanse of British.
 * English east India company had a much superior naval force by 1760. Moreover, they operated from Bombay and Calcutta while the French operated only from Pondicherry.
 * The recall of Dupleix did not prove to be beneficial for the French and there was a dearth of people expert in strategy-making, on the other hand, the British got Robert Clive the master strategist.
 * The French general Count-De-Lally was arrogant and rash towards his own people because of his ill-temper there was no cohesion in the French army. While the British were operating under a unified command of Coote.

title: Regional Powers
content:
> Regional Power of Bengal

Timeline:
Foundation -> 1717 by Murshid Quli Khan
MQK (1717-27) → Shujauddin (1727-) → Sarfaraj Khan (27-39) → Aliwardi Khan (39-56) → War of Succession → Siraj-udaulah (56-57) -> B.o.Plassey (57) → Mir Jafar (57-60) → Mir Qasim (60-63) → B.o.Buxar (64) → T.o.Allahabad (65)
Salient aspects of Bengal:
 * It was the richest province of Mughal India and became further rich in tenure of MQK due to his effective leadership.
   * Bengal had a very effective revenue collection system. MQK preferred few powerful zamindars instead of large number of smaller zamindars for revenue collection. By the time of his death in 1727, 15 powerful zamindars of Bengal were responsible for >60% revenue collection of Bengal.
   * In Bengal there was growing importance of merchant-bankers. They proved as a supportive pillar at each and every level of transaction. They provided cheap loan to the people which further led to build of trust in society.
   * Bengal was unique in the sense that its most important nobility did not came from jagirdari but from merchant banker family. The most important of them were the Jagat Seths.
     * In 1717, Merchant-banker Manickchand supported MQK against mughal emperor Farukhsiyar. As a. token of gratitude, manikchand was given minting rights of bengal. His son fatehchand emerged as the richest person in the contemporary world.
     * Mughal emperor, Rangeela gave him the title of Jagat Seths.
     * Jagat seths build a very trustworthy relationship in Bengal society, as they provided loans of cheaper interest rates. They released Hundi's (Bill of exchange) for higher amount loans which started from 1 lakh rupees till 2 crore rupees.
     * It was popular in Bengal that if Jagat seths collapse, trade in Bengal will also collapse.
   * In 1740s there was a political turmoil in north and northwest Indian due to the invasions of Nadir Shah and Abdaili. At this time oceanic trade stated to thrive in Bengal with renewed interest of Europeans.
   * During the Tenure of MQK many progressive initiatives were taken regarding Agriculture and Manufactring. Bengal emerged as the manufacturing hub of the world. Eg: Dakha -> Textile production hub of India.
   * Bengal had very negligent import demand but it had very high volume of export of manufactured goods which resulted into favorable Balance of Trade with Bengal.
   * The trade in bengal led to huge accumulation of Bullion particularly gold.
   * Trade in bengal was win win situation for every stakeholder eg: Producers->Merchant-Bankers->Europenan traders and rulers. Moreover, European companies needed permission of the Indian rulers to operate in India. To get this they often made Peshkash(nazrana) to Indian rulers.
     

Alivardi khan (1739-1756):
 * He was a very strong economic administrator but not strong military commander. Though the wealth of Bengal increased during his tenure but Bengal was not saved from the raids of Marathas.
 * In between 1745-50, Marathas raided Bengal many a times and they also looted many of the Kothis of jagat Seths. in 1751, Alivardi Khan signed an agreement with Marathas whereby he handed over Odisha to Marathas and also assured 1.25 lakh rupees annual payment to Marathas.
 * After the death of Alivardi khan, war of succession started b/n his grandson {Siraj-udaulah} and daughter {Ghaseeti Begum}. At this point of time many people supported claim of later like Jagat Seths and BEIC but finally Siraj-udaulah came to power.
   

Siraj-Udaulah (1756-1757)

 * After coming to power he had to take a very important decision regarding grant of Monopoly of trade either to B's or F'h. Jagat Seths wanted this monopoly given to BEIC. Young Siraj did not wanted to pay heed to dictat's of Jagat Seths. He rather decided no one will be given monoploy of trade in bengal and bengal will remain open competitive economy. Both jagat seth and company officials were offended by this decision and they started to hatch conspiracy against him.
   

Events leading to Palassy Battle:
 1. During the war of succession B's supported Ghaseeti Begum.
 2. When Siraj Assended the throne French made a Peshkash but not BEIC.
 3. Siraj-udaulah declared Krishnadas as a fugitive but B's gave asylum to him in fort William Calcutta.
 4. On the pretext of renewed fight with French in bengal, B's started to mount guns on the walls of Fort William. When Siraj asked to remove guns from walls they did not followed his orders.

→ Seeing his authority flouted, he decided to launch the offense he marched towards Calcutta from Murshidabad (This was the capital of Bengal)

→ Reaching Fort William, he ordered all the B's to be put into a small cell. Next morning when the door was open only 21 people survived and rest of them died due to suffocation and infighting. B'h historian calls this as Black Hole Tragedy.

# Battle of Palassey (1757)
 * After some point of time, Robert Clive came to Calcutta with a bigger British army. It led to treaty of Alinagar with Siraj in Feb 1757. As per treaty, siraj paid compensation to the british and also restored British privileges in Calcutta. It was not enough for Clive, he was a man of greed and for his greed sky was the limit.
 * He started to create a web of Intrigue in Bengal politics whereby he started to bribe many important officers of Bengal court, which includes Commander in chief Mir Jafar. He was also supported by Jagat Seths.
 * It is B's who initiated the fight by marching towards Murshidabad. Both the armies met at Palassey, where Mir Jafar effected a coup and this battle was finished in few days only. Siraj was captured and later was executed. After his execution, Mir Jafar was appointed as new Nawab of Bengal.
   

Repercussions of Plassey Battle
 1. It increased the British prestige in a single stroke.
 2. Immediately, the B's were paid 2,75,000 as war indemnity.
 3. In between 1757-60, B's were further paid 22.5 million rupees.
 4. Mir Jafar also gave them the zamindari right of 24 paragana.
 5. With the plassey plunder, B's further consolidated their positon in india. The loot was used to fund military campaign in different part of India.
 6. The favorable balance of trade with Bengal now became -ve. The Bengali bullion and money was used by B's to purchase goods from different parts of India and china.
    

Mir Jafar (1757-1760) -> Sponcered Nawab, {the Traitor}
 * The demands of B's was increasing day by day and Mir Jafar felt that he cannot fulfill all the greeds of B's. He started negotiations with Dutch to seek military support. When it was learnt by B's they attacked the dutch position in Bengal i.e. at Hoogly, Chinsura, Bedara. In battle of Bedara (1759) dutch were throughly defeated and next Mir jafar was removed to power.
   

Mir Qasim (1760-1763)
 * After coming to power gave the zamindari power of Mindapore, Chittagong, Bardhaman to B's. He also paid 29 lakh rupees to B's in this duration.
 * He also realised that it is difficult to fulfill the B's demand. To reduce their interference in bengal politics he shifted the capital to Monghyr.
 * He also abolished all the inland duties to favour indian traders.{Highly opposed by B's -> led to 3 successive fights with B's}
 * In 1763, Mir Qasim fled to Awadh to forge an alliance with awadh ruler Suha-Udaulah and Mughla Emperor Shah Alam II

# Battle of Bauxar (1764)
The combined strength of 3 armies was around 70k but there were many segmentary divisions in combined army and moreover there was no central authority. OTOH, the B's army was 1/10th of the combined army but it operated under the central command of captain Munro. The B's army was laced with sophisticated weapons. As a result combined army was thoroughly defeated.

Treaty of Allahabad (1765)
 1. Shah Alam was treated with respect. He was also assured of 26 lakh annual payment by B's.
 2. B's got Diwani right of Bihar, Bengal, and Odisha {Revenue collection rights}.
 3. Awadh became Alliance partner of B's and awadh ruler paid 50 lakh rupees as war indemnity.
 4. Mir Qasim was exiled from Bengal.
 5. Dual system of Administration was started in Bengal Wherby Nizamat function (civil administration) was managed by Bengal nawab appointed by B's. Diwani function was to be looked after by B's.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Regional Power: Mysore

Mysore was the vice-royality of vijaynagar kingdom. After Battle of Talikota (1565), decline of vijaynagar started. At this point of time Mysore emerged as an independent territory carved out form vijaynagar. During the regime of Aurangzeb some of the areas of Mysore came under Mughal captivity. Later after death of him, mysore restored its complete Independence under Wodeyar rulers.

Krishnaraja Wodeyar was the ruler in the initial decade of 18th cen. By 1730s two of his ministers i.e. Nanjraja and Devaraja usurped the power from krishnaraja Wodeyar and started to rule.

Haider Ali (

 * He was a petty soldier in Mysore's army. Though he was illiterate but a man of immense capacity and capability. Fortune was on his side and also he was also highly visionary. Due to his effective functioning he rose to the position of commander in Cheif of Mysore forces. In 1761, he threw Nanjaraja and devaraja from power and installed himself as the ruler of Mysore.
   

Salient features of Mysore:

 1. With the help of french officials, Hyder Ali created a modern arsenal(Weapon making factory) at Dindigul.
 2. He also introduced western methods of training in his army {similar to the French model}
    

He organized his army under Risala System, whereby army was divided into many risalas or units. Each of the Risala had equal number of soldiers, horses, weapons and a commander directly appointed by Hyder Ali. There was clear chain of command starting from the ruler and going up to the soldiers.

 1. Mysore had very effective revenue policy:
    * It followed the mughal model whereby land was Surveryed and classified a.t. its fertility.
    * revenue was collected by salaried officials and it was collected only in cash.
    * very often mysore fiscal policy/revenue policy is considered as Military fiscalism (>50% of revenue was allocated to army)
    * Both Hyder ali and tipu sultan promoted agricultural Manufactring whereby sericulture and textile was promoted. They also repaired the old irrigation channels and also created new ones.
    * They also sent many trade missions to countries like Afghanistan, Iran, turkey and France.
      

Reasons which led to fight of Mysore with its neighbors:

 * In a very short span of time there was rapid territorial expansion of Mysore under Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan. Boundaries of Mysore stretched from Krishna River in north and malabar in south west. Initially, Hyder Ali did not respected the Bufferzones and unilaterally tried to annex the Buffer zone territories held by Polygars and deshmukhs.
   
 * This brought Mysore in confrontation with its neighbors.
   
 * The Francophil policy of Mysore (brotherly love) was highly detested by English.
   
 * In 1785, Tipu Sultan came up with an instruction whereby he banned trade of black pepper and cardamom from the ports in his territory. In 1788, he explicitly banned any kind of trade with BEIC. Company was offended by it.
 * Both HA and TS wanted to create a big Islamic kingdom of south India with a desire to control Maratha politics also.
   

1st Anglo-Mysore War (1767-69)

 * Due to the territorial dispute Maratha attacked on Mysore but very soon Nizam of Hyd and BEIC also joined the war from the side of Maratha.
 * Hyder Ali realized that he cannot fight 3 enemies using his diplomatic strategy he detached Marathas and Nizam from the war. He assured them border dispute redressal from discussion.
 * After detaching the two big powers HA attacked on British area of Madras. B's were caught unaware and they surrendered at this time.
 * This war ended with T.o.Madras, whereby Madras was restored to B's and B's promised to HA that in future war against Mysore by any other power BEIC will come to help HA.
   

2nd Anglo-Mysore War (1780-84)

 * The territorial dispute between Maratha and Mysore was not solved.
 * As such Maratha attacked Mysore in 1771 due to no resolution in Border dispute
 * Hyder Ali expected British support at this time by British did not come to the rescue of Hyder Ali.
 * HA realized that Europeans can't be trusted except French.
 * During American war of independence he started to support french and he also started to provide refuge french forces at Mahe. It led to the outbreak of 2nd war.
 * Initially HA had upper hand in this war but later after his death in 1782, B's started to control the direction of war.
 * Son of his TS carried forwarded the war and he had a big victory against B's in battle of Pollillur. In this battle TS used innovative Mysorian Rockets. which led to his victory.
 * By 1784, this war proved to be inconclusive and ended with t.o.Mangalore. As per the treaty both the sides exchanged prisoners and also restored each others territory.
   

Tipu Sultan

 * He was bold and courageous ruler, often referred as "Tiger of Mysore".
 * He supported French revolution and became member of Jacobian club of france. To display his support he planted a tree of liberty at his capital -> Seringpatnam.
 * He also sought an alliance with Napoleon of France so as the king of Afghanistan Zaman Shah.
 * He also initiated a new system of coinage and calendar in Mysore.
 * He had very strong conviction in his values, when BEIC offered him to join subsidiary alliance he refused by saying
   
   > I would better die like a soldier than a miserable dependent on the infidels, in the list of their pensioned Raja's and Nawab's

3rd Anglo-Mysore War (1790-92)

 * By 1790, dutch were winding up their operations from South India. They sold their properties at Jallikottal and Cannanore to the ruler of Travancore. Since these areas were in Cochin and Cochin was feudatory of TS, hence, Tipu warned Travancore from purchasing the property.
 * When the ruler of Travancore did not paid any heed, TS attacked on Travancore. Very soon, B's joined the war along with Maratha and Nizam form the side of Travancore.
 * As a result TS was thoroughly defeated and this war ended with t.o.Seringpatnam.
 * As per the treaty, half of the Mysorian territory including the areas of Malabar, Barahmahal and Dindigal were taken away by the fighting partners. Moreover 3.3 crore rupee war indemnity was imposed on TS.
   

In between 1792-98, TS started to strengthen his bond with Napoleon of France and Zaman Shah of Afghanistan. In 1798 the new GG of Bengal was Richard Wellesley came with subsidiary Alliance policy.


4th Anglo-Mysore War (1799)
 * Wellesley chargesheeted TS of planning and plotting against BEIC with the help of France and Afghanistan. He gave the option to TS to accept Subsidiary alliance or to be prepared for the war. TS rejected the offer.
 * At this point of time one of the trusted officer of Tipu, Mir Sadiq, treacherously gave all the strategic information about Mysore fort and army to BEIC.
 * In 1799, the 4th war started and it was over within 40 days. TS laid down his life defending his capital Seringpatnam.
 * After the death of Tipu, wodeyar dynasty was reappointed on mysores throne but it was kept under subsidiary alliance system.
