 * Congress ministries tried to implement gandhian measures in respective states like Gandhian education policy, promotion of vegetarianism, protection of cattle's and discouraging intoxicating drinks.
 * Some of the states like UP came up with tenancy reform legislation.
 * Industrial dispute bill was also introduced which was based on cooperation with labour class but not confrontation.

Few blemishes of congress ministries:

 1. Premier of madras Rajagopalachari arrested two left wing congress socialist leaders Yusuf Mehrally and SS Batliwala for giving inflammatory speeches against british authority. He was criticized for behaving like british.
 2. Home minister of Bombay KM Munshi used the services of CID to spy on left wing and communist leaders.

In Sept 1939, WW2 broke out and British authority declared India to be party in WW2 w/o taking connivance of Indian leadership. At this time Gandhi asked a question “How come an enslaved nation like India could be of any help to british against the fascist forces of Hitler?”

After 28 month rule congress ministries tendered their resignation.

# TOWARDS FINAL ACT:

Annual Session of INC 1938 (Haripura):

 * SCB was elected as president of congress in this session. {SVP did not like Bose much due to the personal issue → Court case for the property of vitthal bhai patel}
 * Bose proposed creation of Planning committee to formulate the scheme of industrialization of India. JLN was chosen to be chairman of planning committee of India.
 * In this session Bose further declared his reservation against Gandhi's charkha policy. He rejected gandhian model of village centric model and promotion of small cottage industries.

 * the differences b/n Gandhi and Bose were clearly wide and out in this session.
   
   [api/attachments/WSjdTAqFhdg2/image/image]

Annual Session of INC 1939 (Tripuri):

 * Bose once again announced his candidature for congress president candidature. This time he knew that his candidature will be opposed by Gandhi.
 * Initially Gandhi approached JLN, who suggested name of Maulana Azad Sahib. Maulana Azad refused to contest against SCB. As such Gandhi came up with the name of Dr. Pattabhi Sitaramayya.
 * In presidential elections, Bose defeated Pattabhi Sitaramayya. Gandhi at this time said “this defeat is more mine then him(Dr. Pattabhi Sitaramayya) after all it was me who insisted his name when maulana Azad sahib refused to contest”
 * Gandhi further said “I'm glad on his victory after all Subash babu is not the enemy of the country. He has suffered for it. In his opinion he has the boldest program and policies. in this scenario minority can only wish him best of luck”.
 * immediately GB Pant moved a resolution reposing faith in the old working committee according to the wishes of Gandhi. This resolution got passed but Gandhi did not approved it. He said “He would not impose his choice on Bose and he is free to select his working committee”.
 * Such was the disastrous division in the congress that Bose decided to resign form presidents position. It led to election of Rajendra prasad as new president.
 * Bose created Forward Block to pursue the objective of India's freedom.

Pakistan declaration of ML (March 1940):

 * ML declared formation of separate nation Pakistan by joining geographically continuous areas in NW and east where Muslims are in majority.

August Offer 1940 by Viceroy Linlithgow:

 * by August 1940, Germany made huge advances in ww2 scenario. By this time Belgium, holland , and France has fallen to Germany.
 * It was compulsion of Britain to seek the cooperation of indian leaders.
 * Proposals:
   * Expansion of viceroy executive council to include more indians.
   * Creation of war advisory council, which will also include indian members.
   * After the war is over a representative body is created to frame the constitution of india.
   * No constitutional advancement will happen against the wishes of minority.
 * Later viceroy executive council was expanded and more indians were included in it.

Annual Session of INC 1940 (Ramgarh) → president Maulana Azad Sahib.

 * In this session congress reiterated its position on war that an enslaved nation like India could be of no help of Britain.
 * It asked british authority to accede to indian demands peacefully in this duration o/w when the organization would be fit enough for the purpose it will aim for civil disobedience.

Individual Satyagraha:

 * Gandhi sympathize with efforts of Britain in ww2 against fascist forces of Hitler. At this time he did not wanted to initiate any mass movement because it will embarrass Britain in its just causse.
 * Gandhi wanted to give ample time to british authority to accept indian demands peacefully.
 * But at the same time there was a need to give strong political expression to the public of India.
 * Individual satyagraha was a step in this direction. It was CDM but on personal level. It was all about emphasizing on freedom of speech and expression and also deepening anti-imperial feeling.
 * Satyagrahi's were to be chosen carefully so that their speeches must not lead to violent scenario.
 * Vinoba Bhave was the 1st, JLN was 2nd and Brahma Dutta was the 3rd satyagrahi.

There was a meeting of big leaders of world in Feb 1941, that included British PM Winston Churchill, Chinese leader Chiang-Kai-Shek and US President Roosevelt. In this meeting Roosevelt raised the qns on constitutional reforms in India and he also sympathizes with India's aspirations of freedom.

Moreover there was pressure from other allies on Britain. In this situation Churchill decided to sent Cripps Mission to India. He chose senior labor politician Stafford Cripps, who had very good relationship with indian leaders and he also supported India's aspiration of self rule.

Cripps Mission:

 1. A Dominion of India would be setup. Indians will have option to remain in british commonwealth or they can secede form commonwealth in later times.
 2. Constituent assembly would be created to frame the constitution of India. Members would be elected from british indian provinces and nominated by princely states.
 3. If any of the province is not agreeing to the proposed constitutional scheme they will enjoy the right to sign a separate agreement regarding their future and they can also have a separate constitution. {Blueprint of Partition}
 4. Nothing would be transacted against the wishes of minority.
 5. For the present time defense of India would be in the hands of british authority.

→ Congress objected on Dominion Status, nomination by princely states and blueprint of partition.

Quit India Movement QIM (1942-1947):

 * By 1942, Japan emerged as the most powerful nation in ww2 scenario. It acquired almost all the European colonies in east Asia and SE Asia. Japan occupied Singapore and came closer to NE India.
 * Japan broke the myth of british invincibility in sea warfare. Japan's victory till Andamans created little bit of anxiety in India.
 * At this time Gandhi was also very agitated due to failure of Cripps mission. He said “Leave India to god, if that is too much leave her to anarchy. I would risk it…”
 * On 8th Aug 1942, At Gowalia Tank in Bombay Gandhi addressed the nation.
 * Gandhi appealed to masses to initiate a non-violent CDM, do not pay any heed to the orders of british authority and act as an independent nation.
 * He also gave the slogan “DO or DIE !!”
 * But in the early morning of 9th of Aug, all the major leaders were arrested in a single sweep and they were lodged into different different jails.
 * In this situation, a young leader of congress Aruna Asaf Ali unfurled the indian flag at Gowalia Tank to start QIM. But from its very 1st day movement was leaderless.
 * Since the movement was leaderless it turned violent. Moreover british authority also responded with extreme violence and suppression.
 * Underground activity:
   * Not allowed to function in open, many underground networks started to emerge in various provinces of India.
   * The main leaders of this were Achyut Patwardhan, Biju Patnaik, RP Goeka, JP Narayan, Ram Manohar Lohia, Ram Nandan Mishra, Aruna Asaf Ali, Usha Mehta and Sucheta Keipalini.
   * The most daring activity of underground network was start of congress radio. Aruna Asaf Ali and Sucheta Kripalini were the organizer of radio and Usha Mehta was the announcer.
   
   * Ram Manoharr Lohia regularly broadcasted from congress radio and congress radio operated clandestinely form different different locations in Bombay city.
     
     [api/attachments/P3p7tAHk7dl1/image/image]
   * One of the women industrialist of India, Sumati Morarji helped Achyut Patwardhan, to evade from arrest by giving new car to him on daily basis.
   * The biggest contribution of underground activity was keeping the spirit of QIM in the very severe phase of suppression.

title: The End & Extras...
content:
* QIM led to violence form the side of state so as the masses.
 * At this time B's asked Gandhi to condemn the violence of masses. Gandhi not only refused but went on to say it is Leonine violence of state which have provoked the masses, if they are serious about stopping violence then they must stop their own violence.
 * Gandhi announced 21 day fast against state violence. In the course of fast Gandhi's health started to deteriorate. On one hand anxious nation started to plead for Gandhi's health on the other hand, british authority went ahead with funeral preparation of Gandhi.
 * At this time 3 members of viceroy executive council, MS Aney, NR Sarkar, HP Mody, resigned from viceroy executive council. They said they were party to QIM suppression but they would be not party to Gandhi's death.
 * Parallel governments:
   1. Balliya District of UP by Chity Pandey in Aug 1942: It survived for just 1 month and in this duration they tried to create some departments of administration like health, education etc.
   2. Jatiya Sarkar Tamluk Midnapore : It was formed in Dec 1942 and it survived for more then a year. They also created many department of administration like health, education, agriculture. They created an armed force k/as Vidyut Vahini, which performed relief work during tropical cyclone in bengal.
   3. Prati Sarkar of Satara, MH: It was formed in mid 1943 by two leaders Nana Patil and YB Chaven. It was the longest surviving parallel government. They not only created departments of administration but also Nyaydan Mandal to dispense justice. They also conducted Gandhi marriages (mass marriages with crowd funding)

CR Formula (1944):

 * In 1944, Rajagopalachari proposed a formula to break the deadlock b/n congress and ML which is as follows:
   * for the time being ML must abandon demand of separate nation Pakistan and it must cooperate with Indian national congress in seeking independence of India.
   * After the end of the war a plebiscite shall be held in the areas where Muslims are in majority NW and east to decide whether or not a separate nation would be created.
   * In case of partition, agreements would be made on Finance, defense and all the essential matters.
 * The above terms shall be binding. Only in case of complete transfer of power by british authority.
 * MAJ did not agreed to the said plebiscite provision as such it ended in no result.

Desai-Liaquat Pact (1944):

 * Bholabhai Desai and Liaquat Ali khan of ML met to break the deadlock b/n congress and ML. They agreed that ML will abandon the demand of Pakistan but in return they would be given parity in council of ministers and in the interim government.
 * Moreover, they also agreed that 20% seats would be reserved for depressed classes.

# SHIMLA CONFERENCE AND WAVELL'S OFFER:

 * General elections were about to happen in Britain by the end of 1945. Moreover anti-incumbency factors were very strong in Britain and it was almost sure that conservative party of churchill will be replaced by labor party.
 * Viceroy Wavell was instructed to convene the meeting of major indian leaders to arrive at constitutional solution.
 * All the major leaders of congress including Gandhi were released form Jail and invited to participate in Shimla conference where viceroy Wavell proposed his solution:

Wavell's Offer:

 1. All the members of viceroy ex. council will be indians governor general and commander in chief of forces.
 2. Creation of an interim govt within the premise of GoI Act 1935.
 3. In council of ministers, parity would be given to H's and M's.
 4. No constitutional development will happen against the wishes of minority.
 5. After the war is over discussion on new constitution will happen.
 6. viceroy will enjoy veto power on the aid and advise of ministers on important matters.

# AZAD HIND FAUZ & INA:

 * The original Idea of INA was proposed by Rash Bihari Bose.
 * He negotiated with Japanese leaders to give prisoners of war of indian origin to create INA and later INA would be guided by Subash Chandra Bose.
 * By Sept 1942, idea of INA came into reality due to the efforts of Capt. Mohan Singh in Singapore.
 * In early 1943, Bose arrived in SE Asia and he took complete control of INA.
 * Bose created temporary GoI in exile.
 * He also crated Azad Hind Bank and Azad Hind Radio.
 * In July 1944, Bose addressed the nation through Azad hind radio, he seeked blessings of Gandhi and called him “Father of nation”
 * Bose created different regiments of INA, named after the major nationalist of india like gandhi, Nehru, Maulana Azad and regiment named after himself and a women regiment named rani Laxmi bai.
 * Bose also gave a slogan “Tum mujhe khoon do, mai thume Azadi Dunga” → “If you do desire freedom be prepared to wade through your blood”
 * Bose started the dreaded campaign of INA i.e. Imphal and Kohima campaign in NE India with the help of southern branch of Japanese army but unfortunately his campaign ended in disaster.
 * INA was outnumbered in 1/10 ratio by british indian forces. Moreover Japan surrendered unconditionally after nuclear bombardment in Hiroshima and Nagasaki.
 * As such INA also surrendered in Singapore. Large number of INA soldiers/officers were brought in India for trial.
 * OTOH, Bose died in a mysterious plane crash at Taiwan capital Taipei.

INA Trials:

 * INA officers were brought to India for open court trials. At this time congress created INA defence committee with the leaders like JLN, Bholabhai Desai, TB Sapru, KN Katju, Lt. Col. Horilal, Asaf Ali.
 * During Delhi trials 3 INA officers were given punishment who were Prem Sehegal, Gurabaksha Dillon, and Shahnawaz Hussain. This punishment led to huge protest and movement in the different parts of country.
 * Meanwhile during the trials 3 upsurges happened:
   1. Nov 1945, during Calcutta Trial at Dalhousie Chowk
   2. 11th Feb 1946 during trials at Deshpriya Park Calcutta when INA officer Rashid Ali was given 7 years punishment
   3. 18th Feb 1946 in Royal Indian Navy of Bombay.

RIN Revolt:

 * On 18th Feb 1946, 1100 Indian ratings of HMIS-Talwar announced strike against racial discrimination and unpalatable food was served for many days.
 * Moreover one of the rating BC Dutta shouted britishers quit India. After which he was thoroughly mistreated.
 * Revolt of HMIS Talwar further reached to all the other ships docked at Gateway of India and it further inspired revolt of Calcutta and royal indian navy Karachi.
 * This episode is regarded as last nail in the coffin of british rule in India

# INDEPENDENCE WITH PARTITION:

On 19th Feb 1946, British PM Clement Atlee announced that he is going to send cabinet mission to India with 3 officers i.e. Stafford Cripps, AV Alexander and Pethick Lawrence.

Cabinet Mission (1946):

 1. It proposed the creation of interim govt for transitional period and creation of constituent assembly of India to frame constitution fo India.
 2. On paper it rejected creation of new nation Pakistan.
 3. It proposed creation of strong federation and a weak center.
 4. It divided stated into 3 groups
    1. Group A: Hindu majority states like UP, CP, Bihar, Odisha, Bombay and madras. it will also include commissionary provice of Delhi, Ajmer and coorg.
    2. Group B: Muslim majority states in NW which includes Punjab, nwfp, and Sindh. It will also include commissionry province of Baluchistan.
    3. Group C: Muslim majority states in east i.e. Assam and Bengal
 5. Congress inquired about nature of groupings i.e. compulsory or optional. to this B's replied it is compulsory. As a result congress said that they will change this provision with their majority in constituent assembly. At this time Muslim League gave a call for Direct Action Day on 16th Aug 1946.
    * It was an unfateful day which led to killing of more than killing of >5000 people in Calcutta itself.

On 2nd of Sept, Interim government was created under PM JLN. ML boycotted to join the government but later in Oct 1946, viceroy Wavell brought 5 members of ML to joint the govt who were Liaquat Ali Khan, Gajanafar Khan, Rashid Ali Ahmad, Jogendranath Mondal, II Chundrigar.

But the members of ML rather than cooperation created obstruction in interim govt.

On 9th Dec 1946, Constituent assembly was created to frame the constitution of India. In Constituent assembly also ML created hinderances.

In Feb 1947, once again british PM Clement Atlee announced that british will start transfer of power by June 1948. But in India instances of communal violence increased as such in march 1947 Lord Louis Mountbatten was sent as last Viceroy of India.

Mountbatten was given power to decide the matter then and there. Initially he came up with Dickie Bird Plan aka Plan Balkan. As per this plan all the indian provinces and princely states were to be given successor state status. JLN heavily opposed this plan and said it will lead to balkanization of India. Immediately this plan was scrapped.

3rd June Plan / Mountbatten Plan:

 * It proposed the creation of two dominion i.e. India and Pakistan.
 * Mountbatten also decided the newly emerging Pakistan will also be partitioned so as to keep maximum unity with indian union.
 * As per this plan Hindu part of bengal will join India and so as Hindu and Sikh part of Punjab will also join India.
 * He also created boundary commission under the chairmanship of sir Radcliff.
 * 3rd June plan of Mountbatten was introduced as India independence bill in british parliament which got passed on 18th July 1947.
 * As per this act India's freedom day was 15th Aug and Pakistan's 14th Aug.
 * Boundary commission report was already available by 10th Aug but it was made public after independence so that responsibility of communal killing will not fall on british heads.

# DEVELOPMENT OF EDUCATION:

 * Right from the beginning B's were of the view that there is a need to educate some indians who are brown in colour but british in behavior.
 * In 1781, Warren Hastings created Calcutta madrasa to impart traditional Islamic education.
 * In 1791, Jonathan Duncan created Sanskrit college at Banaras to impart traditional Sanskrit medium education.
 * By 1800, Wellesley created Civil Servant Training College at Fort William Calcutta. But later it was disapproved by court of directors and 1806 they created a separate civil servant college at Haileybury in England.
 * Charter Act 1813, allocated 1 lakh rupees for the promotion of education in India. But it led to debate and discussion regarding mode of education. They got divided into two groups:
   * Anglicist → who favored English medium education
   * Orientalist → who favored vernacular medium education
 * No money was released till 1820. After 1820s little bit of money is released as per instrument of instruction.
 * In 1833, GG of India William Bentick appointed Macauley committee to resolve this issue. He favored Anglicist view. He also recommended official language of India {Persian} must be replaced by English.

Downward Filtration Theory (Trickle Down Effect):

 * William Bentick was of the view that 1L rupees is a meager amount to educate all indians.
 * He said with this allocated money only few indians can be educated. In turn it would be responsibility of indians to educate the masses.
 * In this way education will trickle down to indians in a gradual way.

Woods Dispatch (1854):

 * SoS Charles Wood gave his famous dispatch on education in India in 1854.
 * It rejected downward filtration theory and maintained that it is responsibility of british authority to educate indians.
 * The provisions were very progressive and it started to be regarded as mangna carta of education in India.
 * Provisions:
   1. It created 3 levels of educaiton along with different meduum of instruction.
      1. Primary educaion in vernacular medium.
      2. Secondary education in Anglo-vernacular medium
      3. Higher education in English medium through established universities in presidency towns. As a result three universities were created i.e. Bombay, madras and Calcutta
   2. It called for teachers training, vocational education, and womens education.
   3. It further emphasized on establishment of more colleges of engineering and technology so as new Agricultural research institutes (Thomson college of Civil Engineering, Roorkee 1847 was the 1st engineering college of India and Agricultural research institute at Pusa was the 1st agricultural research institute estd in April 1905, originally its name was imperial agricultural institute)
   4. It said education in public institute must be secular in outlook.

Hunter Commission of 1882:

 * It supported continuation of primary education in vernacular. But it prescribed secondary education must be diversified into two types i.e. vocational and literary education

Thomas Raleigh Committee:

 * It was constituted by Viceroy Curzon in 1902.
 * On the recommendation of this committee, the Indian Universities Act was passed in 1904 to provide a regulatory framework for Indian universities.

Sadler Commission 1916:

 * This committee was appointed to review the functioning of Calcutta University and make recommendations for its better functioning.
 * But, its recommendations proved to be equally beneficial to the entire educational sector of India.
 * For the first time, two Indians were part of this committee, i.e. Ashutosh Mukherjee, and Zia-Uddin-Ahmad. (Later Ashutosh Mukherjee became the Vice-Chancellor of Calcutta University.)
 * Recommendations (very progressive in nature → tf adopted all indian educational scenario):
   * It suggested bringing efficiency in secondary education for better functioning of the universities.
   * It proposed 12 years of schooling and a 3-year degree course.
   * It maintained that the boards for High School and Intermediate education must be separated.
   * It further proposed the establishment of more new universities to cater educational needs of indians. As a result, in between 1916 to 1921, seven new universities were created which are:
     * 1. BHU (Banaras Hindu University) → 1916
     * 2. Aligarh
     * 3. Lucknow
     * 4. Patna
     * 5. Dacca
     * 6. Mysore
     * 7. Hyderabad (Osmania University)

Hartog Commission 1929:

 * It suggested bringing efficiency in education through already established educational institutes.
 * It forbade the creation of new educational institutions in India.

Wardha Scheme of Education (1937):

 * It was proposed by Gandhi and the elaborate syllabus of Wardha education was created by Zakir Hussain.
 * It was also known as “Nai Taleem.”
 * It promoted common basic minimum education to all indians and also the promotion of values of ethics and morality.
 * Gandhi promoted traditional hand on learning and this scheme was implemented by many of the congress provincial governments.

Sargeant Plan of Education (1944):

 * It was the last proposal of the British before Independence.
 * It wanted to bring universal basic education to Indians but unfortunately, it did not take off.

S Radhakrishnan Committee 1948:

 * The committee was constituted to suggest measures for Indian universities.
 * As per the recommendations of this committee, University Grants Commission was created to provide a regulatory framework and financial assistance to Indian universities.
 * Later in 1956, UGC Act was passed and it became a statutory autonomous body.
 * UGC decides the pattern of funding and regulation of indian universities.

# PHASES OF COLONIALISM IN PRINCELY STATES:

→ Phase of BEIC struggle for equality (1740-65):

 * In 1740s, British started to fight with French to increase their political influence in India and so as to get greater milage in Carnatic.
 * Though French focused only on Carnatic, british opened another war front in bengal.
 * After battle of Plassey 1757, british received huge money from Plassey plunder which was utilized in completely defeating French in 3rd Carnatic war.
 * Moreover after battle of bauxar with treaty of Allahabad britishers got diwani rights of Bihar, Bengal and Odisha.
 * It brought british to an equal status with indian princely states.

→ Phase of Ring Fence (1765-1813):

 * After battle of Bauxar, a nascent british empire got created, which needed protection form both external and internal.
 * They started to create a ring fence around their newly created territory.
 * In this regard, the first initiative was Effective control policy of Cornwallis. Later the most useful tool in this regard was subsidiary alliance policy of Wellesley.

→ Phase of subordinate isolation (1813-1858):

 * In 1813, the newly appointed GG M Hastings declared that british power is paramount and supreme in India and indian princely states are subordinate to british.
 * He signed agreement with indian princely states by making them subordinate.
 * Later, Dalhousie brought Doctrine of Lapse which de-recognized indian princely states w/o a successor form direct blood.
 * In this way british authority isolated indian powers.
 * It further led to resentment and anger in India and finally resulted in to revolt of 1857.

→ Phase of Subordinate Union (1858-1935):

 * After suppression of 1857 revolt, B's felt the need of cooperation with indian princely states.
 * Queen Victoria proclamation of 1858 disclaimed any intention of conquest in India and further assured indian princely states that their rights would be respected.
 * In 1876, queen Victoria took the title of Empress of India, very often british authority conducted grand durbars to display unity with indian princely states.
 * In 1921, Chamber of princes was created, it was a platform where princely states can discuss their grievances with british authority.
 * In 1927, Butler commission was constituted to suggest measures regarding how to streamline relationship b/n princely states and british authority.

→ Phase of Equal federation (1935):

 * GoI Act 1935 proposed creation of All India federation comprising of british indian provinces and indian princely states.
 * Creation of federation was conditional on fulfilment of two criteria which never happened and federation never 
   became operational.

# PRINCELY STATES MOVEMENT:

In 1920, When NCM started in british indian provinces, people of princely states also started mass movement. For this purpose they started to create Praja Mandal.

In 1927, all India state people conference was created by ML Kothari, JR Abhyankar, Balwant Rai. They persuaded congress to merge national movement with princely states movement.

In 1929, JLN as the president of Lahore session this idea of merger but congress rejected it. Once again in 1939, during All India state people conference at Ludhiana, JLN proposed the merger of two movements which finally happened during QIM.

# PEASANTS ORGANIZATION AND MOVEMENTS IN 20TH CENTURY:

 1. UP Kisan Sabha:
    * It was the 1st Modern peasant organization of 20th century founded by MMM, Indira Narayan Dwivedi and Gauri Shankar Mishra.
    * They adopted the legal and constitutional methods to address the problems of peasants. They did not favored participation in mass movements or popular agitations.
    * When NCM started some of its leaders wanted to join it. as a result it led to creation of Awadh Kisan Sabha in 1920 by Gauri Shankar Mishra, Kedarnath Pandey, Mata Badal Pandey and Baba Ramchandra.
    * They participated in NCM and also supported Gandhi on all the issues.
 2. Eka Movement:
    * It was initiated by a local leader Madari Pasi in 3 districts of UP i.e. Sitapur, Bahraich, and Hardoi.
    * This movement was against high rate of taxes imposed by British authority on peasants.
    * They took pledge of river ganga that they will not pay high amount of taxes.
 3. Moplah Rebellion:
    * Moplah's were the peasants of north Kerala. In this area taxes were collected by Hindu Polygars (british agent)
    * Moplah's organized a protest under the leadership of their priest Ali Musaliar. When British arrested Ali Musaliar, a rumor spread that not only he has been arrested but also famous mosque of Mumbrat had been destroyed by british.
    * In this situation, moplahs organized a rebellion under the leadership of Kun Hanad Haji. Though it started as an agrarian crisis movement but in later phase, it tool communal overtone when moplahs started to attack on houses of hindu polygars.
    * As a result a big british force came in area and this rebellion was brutally suppressed.

After CDM was called off, peasants movements restarted in many parts of India.

 1. All India Kisan Sabha:
    * It was created in 1935 by Swami Sahajanand Sarswati and NG Ranga to highlight the grievances of peasants in a legal constitutional way.
 2. In Surma valley of Assam, Karuna Sindhu Roy initiated a peaceful peasant movement (1935)
 3. In Odisha, Utkal Kisan Sabha initiated a peaceful peasant movement under the leadership of malti Chaudhary 1935
 4. Baksht Movement:
    * Baksht land are those which occupancy tenants have lost due to non payment during the depression years.
    * This movement was initiated by Rahul Sanskritayayana, Karyananadan Sharma and Swami Sahajananad Saraswati in Bihar in b/n 1935-40.
    * It was partly successful and many of the tenants got back their occupancy lands.

Tebhaga Movement (1940-1947)

 * It was the most successful movement before the independence of India.
 * It was operational in entire bengal and officially supported by CPI.
 * in 1946, this movement got intensified and peasants maintained that they will only pay 1/3rd of demand by british authority.

# DEVELOPMENT OF PRESS:

 * Portuguese, in 1557, brought Printing Press to India and it was meant for the publication of the Bible.
 * In 1780, James Augustus Hickey {Irishmen} came up with the first newspaper of India “Bengal Gazette”.
 * He was very critical of the policies of the British, as such, he was put into jail {exposed corruption of Warren Hastings}. But, it promoted journalism in India. And many such newspapers came to reality.
 * Right from the beginning, British authorities were of the view that the press must be regulated.
 * In 1799, Wellesley came up with the Press Censorship Act, which maintained that it is necessary to take the permission of British authorities before publishing any article.
 * Later, in 1823, John Adams came up with the Press Regulation Act, which said that any article published against British authorities will lead to confiscation of the press and its properties.
 * RRR opposed both acts.
 * The Indian press was relieved from all the restrictions in 1835 by Charles Metcalfe, who was considered the “Liberator of the Indian Press”.
 * The next 40 years were good for the growth of Indian journalism. (1835-76) {Oldest surviving English newspaper → Times of India 1838 (Bombay Times)}
 * In 1878, Viceroy Lytton enacted Vernacular Press Act.
   * As per the Act, the District Collector was given the power to review the functioning of the vernacular press under his jurisdiction.
   * He had the power to confiscate the articles and the property of the press.
   * It popularly came to be known as the Gagging Act of the Press.
   * In one such case, Amrita Bazar Patrika overnight changed its publication from the Bengali Language to English to evade confiscation
   * The Vernacular Press Act was later repealed by Viceroy Lord Ripon in 1882.
 * 1882-1905 → Again growth of Indian Journalism. {The hindu, Indian Express, Indian Opinion, Bombay Mirror, Indu Prakash, Commonweal, Keashri, Mahratta, Bengalee}
 * During Swadeshi and Boycott Movement, the British government came up with:
   * The Newspaper Incitement to Offence Act, 1908, and the Indian Press Act, 1910 restricted the circulation of nationalist ideology.
   * Both the Acts were completely operational during the Non-Cooperation Movement.
   * Finally, due to the persuasion of Tez Bahadur Sapru, it was repealed in 1925.
 * During the Civil Disobedience Movement, the Press Emergency Act, of 1931 was implemented to restrict nationalist activities.
 * After the independence of India, the government introduced Press Objectionable Matter Bill in 1951
   * It was contested by the opposition and the All India Press Association.
   * As such GS Rajyadhaksha Committee was created which suggested the scrapping of the bill.

# RISE OF LEFT IN INDIA:

Socialism:

 * Socialism started to shape in India by the third decade of the 20th century.
 * Jawahar Lal Nehru was the greatest representative of socialist thought in India along with Bose.
 * In 1934, the All India Socialist Congress was created within the fold of Congress by leaders like JP Narayan, Acharya Narendra Dev, and Minoo Masani.
 * The impact of this organization was clearly visible in the 1936 elections and the Congress manifesto.
 * After the formation of Congress ministries, the socialist goals were fulfilled by many Congress governments.

Communism:

 * The Communist Party of India was created by MN Roy in 1920 at Tashkent.
 * The formal announcement of this party in India was made by Satya Bhakt in 1924 at Kanpur.
 * Other Communist parties:
   * Labour Swaraj Party of INC: It included leaders like Muzaffar Ahmad, Hemant Kumar Sarkar, and Qazi Nazrul Islam.
   * Worker and Peasant Party of India: This organization was created after the merger of the following communist parties:
     * 1. Congress Swaraj Party of Bombay.
     * 2. Labour Kisan Party of Madaras.
     * 3. Kirti Kisan Party of Punjab.
   * Dr. Ambedkar was associated with the Worker and Peasant Party of India.
 * Right from the beginning, British authorities did not want the spread of communism in India.
 * They started creating conspiracy cases against communist leaders.
   1. Peshawar Conspiracy Case of 1922. • Many communist leaders were arrested who were trying to enter India through Central Asia.
   2. Kanpur-Bolshevik Conspiracy Case 1924: • It led to the arrest of 4 prominent Communist leaders who were, Nazrul Islam, SA Dang, Nalini Gupta, and Shaukat Usmani.
   3. Merrut Conspiracy Case, 1929: • It led to the arrest of 32 prominent Communist leaders including 3 British Communist leaders: Ben Bradley, Phillip Spratt, and Lester Hutchinson.
 * CPI was declared an illegal party in 1934.
 * After the outbreak of World War 2, CPI declared it to be an imperialist war.
 * When Germany invaded Communist Russia in 1941, CPI changed its stance and declared its support for British authority, and World war as a people’s war.
 * In return for support, the CPI has declared a legal party and it did not participate in any of the national movements from 1942 onwards.

# RISE OF TRADE UNIONS

 * The first cotton mill in India was created in Bombay in 1853.
 * The first jute mill was created at Rishra near Calcutta in 1855.
 * In 1858, Dinbandhu Mitra wrote bengali play “Nil Darpan”
 * Later in the 1870s, Shasipada Banarjee wrote “Shramajeebi” which highlighted the plight of workers.
 * NM Lokhandey in 1891 created the first organization for the workers known as the Bombay Mills and Mill Hand Association. {1st Trade Union}
 * In the 20th century, it was Lala Lajpat Rai who facilitated the creation of the All India Trade Union Congress in 1920.
 * After the death of Lala Lajpat Rai, a division started lurking in AITUC.

All-India-Trade-Union-Congress --> All-India-Trade-Union-Federation
 All-India-Trade-Union-Congress --> Red-Trade-Union

 * The communist faction wanted affiliation with the international organization based in Moscow and the socialist faction wanted affiliation with a Geneva-based international organization.
 * It led to a schism in AITUC and the creation of the Red Trade Union and the All India Trade Union Federation.
 * During the outbreak of World War 2, the two factions got united due to the efforts of Acharya Narendra Dev and JP Narayan.

# RISE OF CAPITALIST CLASS IN INDIA:

 * The capitalist class started to emerge in India during the course of the National movement.
 * Swadeshi movement gave a big impetus to the cotton textile industry, iron steel industry, and chemical industry.
 * During the outbreak of World War 1, the Indian manufacturing sector further benefitted due to the cut in production in Europe.At this time ailing Tata Steel industry was given a lifeline when the British gave them the Mesopotamian Rail Wagon Project. • Initially, the capitalist class was not in the favour of the national movement, and as a whole, they did not support Swadeshi and Non-cooperation movement. • In fact, Purushottam Das Tandan maintained that he is an enemy of Noncooperation. • After the end of NCM, the capitalist class decided to create their first All-India organization in 1927, i.e. FICCI (Federation of Indian Chamber of Commerce and Industry). • Its objectives were as follows: • They supported the nationalization of Indian industry. • Redistribution of benefits among masses. • Extensive support to freedom struggle under the leadership of Gandhi. • In 1944, they came up with the Bombay Plan, where they further declared their support for the nationalization of banking, financial institutions, and corporatization of financial institutions.
